name: Gobi Aloo[cauliflower and Potatoes]
recipeIngredient: 1/2 large onion sliced thinly into long, thin slices
recipeInstructions:
1. Begin by boiling the potatoes in a saucepan until tender. Boil them for at least 15 minutes, then turn off the heat and let them stand in the water.
2. Cut the cauliflower into small bite-sized pieces (about 1 inch cubes), discarding most of the stem pieces. Rinse and drain the cauliflower in a colander.
3. Heat oil in a large skillet until very hot. Add mustard seeds and wait for them to pop, then add bayleaf, cardamom pods, and cloves.
4. Stir around for a while, then add onions. Cook until they start to turn golden, before adding the remaining spices (except turmeric).
5. Add the cauliflower to the skillet and fry in the oil and spices for 2 minutes. Cut the potatoes into bite-sized pieces and add them to the skillet as well.
6. Stir the vegetables under medium heat for another few minutes. Add half a cup of water, reduce heat to low, cover the skillet, and let it cook for 5 minutes.
7. Check the tenderness of the vegetables; if they are still hard, add an additional quarter cup of water and cover again for 5 more minutes.
8. Salt to taste and serve.
